What is up friends? I wanted to give you a little bit of a template walkthrough and the example we have of the slice of life closing post. So closing post or closing email can be used as either, but essentially it's something you're going to want to do about three hours till your program or your offer or your launch closes. So once again, this is a template that has been built exclusively around coaching safety, which is what we spoke about in the main lesson. Of course, it adds the urgency while also softening it a little bit. So it's got a bit of a disarming feel to it. That's the point and an authentically disarming twist to it. It's not something we're trying to engineer. It's not something we're trying to manipulate. It's essentially giving our very real perspective and our real experience. And that's what I was doing here. So what we're doing is we're overcoming this whole kind of like defense mechanism, where we feel like enrollment is a zero sum game, where we're getting pressured and we're disempowered and we're going to give our money and give our investment to somebody else. And essentially, we end up feeling like the conquest at the end of the day, not someone that just got into this fruitful, co-creative relationship. We feel like, okay, we've been conquered. And that's something we want to defend age. No one wants to feel conquered into a sale. So this is about disarming that by really sharing our relationship to them, our relationship to the idea of the launch, our relationship to what happens after it and giving them that very real slice of life of our experience on the other side of the screen. So I'll read it. I'll point out a few notes unpacking what I'm doing as I'm reading it. But here it goes. So this was the subject line of both the offer post as well as the closing email. So three ish hours till AI closes. Here are my epic post launch plans. So within that, you have a bit of a curiosity loop. These epic post launch plans, right? And it's kind of like playing on this idea of how people tend to have all these crazy celebrations lined up. So as a creator, I've done close to 20 launches of my own stuff. But as a consumer, I've engaged well over 10 times that number. And it's always allowed me to see creators who would use these last few hours to line up the champagne, count the stripe notifications and salivate over the Tesla Model X order page waiting for them on an open Chrome tab, sometimes quite literally documenting that on their Facebook or Instagram feeds, which kind of makes you ask the question, what's their real conquest, their gaudy launch numbers or your success? If it was the latter, then the celebration would be deferred to weeks or months later. And once you the actual client or partner are actually winning and succeeding through the program. So here's this kind of like inarguable reframe, right? Like we're looking for these head nod moments of like, yeah, you know what, that really bothers me too. Yeah, thanks for calling this out. And this is stuff that once again can be faked. So track your own experience in your market in your industry, what kind of bothers you about it and position against it, take a stand against something even in these quote unquote, final urgency driven emails, you can always be taking a stand against something that your audience doesn't appreciate either, and gain trust through that final little countdown. So as we enter the final three hour countdown, I'll offer a reminder, despite the abundance of emails this past week, it's not about our launch, never has been, it's about what happens after. That's where our concern is, that's where that's listening to what we're doing all this for, right? It's just to open up the possibility of the container after the launch where we get to do the real work. So it's about what happens after here's my very non insta worthy plan over the next three hours as we wrap this party up. And then this is the slice of life moment, right? This disarming you are a human, you're not here to conquer anybody, you have a relatable life, and you're just trying to like move on with your evening and get to the good stuff with them later in the program itself. So this is real, this is essentially what I was doing in those final three hours. I'm going to read goodnight construction site to my son multiple times. I can memorize that actually I have memorized that I could totally narrate it off the top of my head right now, but I'm going to spare you the annoyance of reading goodnight construction site. Bruce, I'm poo air. I think I got that right. T and then a shout out to Matt Brown, who gave me an incredible crash course on the beauty and awesomeness and history of pure poo air tea. Sit out on my porch, listen to Bo Ho chill on Spotify and be on standby for any last minute questions. And you can DM me here. So kind of a little bit of a call to action there. And then most importantly, read through the intro post of our 30 plus AI members, senior names, faces and stories and offering a prayer of gratitude and requesting from the powers that be that everyone is served to the fullest and that Phil and I are equipped to do it. Because once the launch is done, the real work begins. So this is really the truth thesis of this email of this communication that disarms this fear, this lingering fear that a lot of people have in this space, that they're the conquest, right, that this is they're just essentially serving the magnitude of your launch that you're going to brag about a week later and create courses on how you essentially boosted your numbers on them, you know. And yeah, this is about kind of like disarming that disarming that fear that a lot of us still carry, including myself. So yes, there will be relief. Yes, there will be a sense of accomplishment, but the whiskey clinks won't be between me and Phil. They'll be between you and us. And there's no one I'd rather click with. People have an appreciation. And yeah, just a little bit of a picture that summarized what we were doing here. I can fit it all into the frame. But at least I got three and four there. And I guess five, which is pretty good. So yeah, that's really it. That's the crux of the post. As you can see, it got quite a lot of reach, quite a lot of comments, quite a lot of engagement, more engagement than one would typically expect from, you know, a closing urgency driven reminder, right? And then we got all sorts of cool things, right? We got, you know, folks like Jin, the amazing Jin Walker, stating that she's in, right? And then we have little phone conversations around that, some love from our good friend, Edie, you know, Matt, taking his tutorials on Tee a little bit further. Brittany also loving the Poo RT. So yeah, a lot of fun stuff here. And then just as a little bit of a hack. So when we initially posted this, we didn't drop all these links here, right? So we kind of let the post take on a bit of its own life. And then really in those closing, I think it was like in the closing hour or the closing 90 minutes, we had Phil kind of just like link bomb this post, right? So that whoever is seeing it at that point has all the resources at their disposal to essentially make a decision, right? So we linked the Google Doc sales letter. We linked our call calendars, right? If anyone needed to book a call with myself or Phil to really gain final clarity, and then straight to the cart for whoever was ready in that moment. So that is essentially the template for this three hour slice of life closing post slash email. And it's one that I think you should have a lot of fun with, right? This takes maybe 10 minutes to write because you're literally just kind of documenting what happens after and sharing your stance that this isn't about the launch. This isn't about the numbers. This isn't about you conquering them into a sale. It's really about all the awesomeness that comes later. Cool. So I encourage you to do this. This one was a lot of fun. It got a lot of traction. It got a lot of sales in those closing hours. Of course, that's difficult to attribute to a single email or a single sales sales offer post alone. Because a lot of people do wait until those last few hours anyway. So I'm not going to over attribute it, but it was effective. It was fun and it got a great response both on our email list as well as in our group. So that's it. This one's here for you. And I encourage you to work that both as a offer post with three ish hours left as well as an email that goes out to your list. So that's it for now. I'll catch you in the next video. Bye for now.
